notes: ITOTORI-239 v0.3 update (was ITOTORI-238). Single (modelId, providerId) primary pair pinned for every stage of the agentic loop; alternateProviders[] now lists FIVE evidence-validated fallback pairs (deepinfra from ITOTORI-238 + wafer, digitalocean, morph, atlas-cloud from ITOTORI-239). The expansion was forced by UTSUSHI-231 retry 7, where BOTH fireworks (primary) AND deepinfra (the sole alternate) returned HTTP 429 simultaneously, wiping the entire agentic-loop bundle. Each new alternate was validated against Trevor's account on 2026-06-26 with a ~200-token translation-style prompt (vs the 16-token ITOTORI-238 toy call); each evidence probe isolated the candidate provider so the response could confirm that specific upstream answers under the ZDR gate (provider.zdr=true + provider.data_collection=deny) — see docs/openrouter-integration-evidence/2026-06-26-itotori-239.json. That per-provider isolation is an evidence-gathering technique, NOT the runtime wire posture: at runtime itotori sends provider.order=[providerId] + provider.allow_fallbacks=true + provider.zdr=true + provider.data_collection=deny and OpenRouter routes within the ZDR allow-list (ITOTORI-241), recording whichever provider actually served. failoverPredicate is the literal 'http_429_from_primary': the orchestrator advances to the next EXPLICIT alternate pair ONLY when the primary returns HTTP 429. Any other failure (provider_response_invalid, non-429 provider_http_error, capability_unsupported, etc.) surfaces immediately — the driver's silent swap to an alternate is forbidden (audit-focus 3). This driver-side explicit-alternate failover is DISTINCT from OpenRouter-side automatic fallback (provider.allow_fallbacks=true), which lets OR route within the ZDR allow-list per call; pair_mismatch is no longer a failure mode (that guard was deleted — provider identity is not a failure axis). Order is intentional: deepinfra first (cheapest at ~$0.0011/Mtok prompt, validated 2026-06-26), then wafer/digitalocean/morph/atlas-cloud (all ~$0.13-0.40/Mtok prompt). The enUsSentinel substring is woven into the translation prompt so the patched Seen.txt's replay produces a TextLine whose bodyUtf8 contains the substring verbatim. ASCII-only by deliberate choice: the leading SJIS bracket required by the KAIFUU-191 lexer is wrapped around the sentinel by the patch step, not by the prompt. Per-stage zdr / fallbackModels / seed / maxPriceUsd are intentionally omitted: the parser fills in canonical defaults. Operator-level zdr downgrades require OPENROUTER_ZDR_DOWNGRADE; no file-level escape hatch.
